---
title: "Curriculum Vitae"
toc: false
---

::: {.cv-header}
# Victor Frankenstein

<div class="cv-subtitle">Computational Neurobiologist · UK Dementia Research Institute</div>

<div class="cv-links">
{{< include ../_partials/links.html >}}
</div>
:::

---

## Summary

Computational neuroscientist studying the molecular mechanisms of
neurodegeneration, with a focus on how misfolded proteins propagate
through neural circuits.

My research combines experimental neuroscience with computational
analysis of large biological datasets, including spatial transcriptomics
and imaging data. I develop reproducible analysis pipelines to understand
how small molecular failures eventually lead to large-scale neuronal loss.

While my earlier scientific ambitions may have involved reanimating
lifeless tissue, my current work focuses on understanding how neurons
can be kept alive for as long as possible.

---

## Employment

**UK Dementia Research Institute — Research Associate** *(2021–Present)*  
- Lead computational analysis of spatial transcriptomics datasets investigating tau pathology in Alzheimer's disease.  
- Developed reproducible pipelines for analysing neurodegenerative disease data across multiple research projects.  
- Collaborate with experimental neuroscientists to integrate molecular data with brain imaging and pathology.

**Transylvanian Institute of Experimental Biology — Postdoctoral Researcher** *(2018–2021)*  
- Studied molecular mechanisms underlying neuronal degeneration and protein aggregation.  
- Designed experimental and computational workflows to analyse large biological datasets.  
- Contributed to interdisciplinary collaborations combining molecular biology and computational modelling.

---

## Selected projects & achievements

- **Mapping tau propagation across neural circuits:** Developed computational workflows for spatial transcriptomics analysis revealing vulnerable neuronal populations in Alzheimer's disease.  
  *(Evidence: analysis pipeline, public dataset, preprint manuscript)*

- **Standardising measurements of protein aggregation:** Built an open-source image analysis pipeline for quantifying tau pathology across multiple research labs.

- **Reproducible neuroscience workshops:** Designed and delivered training sessions helping experimental researchers adopt reproducible computational practices.

---

## Skills

**Programming & tools:** R, Python, Bash, Git, Quarto  
**Data & analysis:** Spatial transcriptomics, imaging analysis, neurodegenerative disease datasets  
**Workflows & reproducibility:** Reproducible pipelines, version control, open science practices  
**Communication & teaching:** Workshops, interdisciplinary collaboration, scientific communication  
**Languages:** English (fluent), German (working knowledge)

---

## Education

**PhD in Molecular Neuroscience — University of Ingolstadt** *(2014–2018)*  
Focus: Molecular mechanisms of neuronal degeneration and protein aggregation.

**MSc in Experimental Biology — University of Ingolstadt** *(2012–2014)*

---

## Awards & fellowships

- Early Career Neuroscience Fellowship — European Society for Neurobiology (2020)  
- Young Investigator Award — International Conference on Neurodegeneration (2019)

---

## Selected publications

- Frankenstein, V. (2025). *Mapping tau-vulnerable cell populations using spatial transcriptomics.* bioRxiv.  
- Frankenstein, V., Igor, I. (2024). *Network models of pathological protein propagation.* Journal of Computational Neuroscience.

---

## Outreach & teaching

- **Workshop:** Reproducible Computational Neuroscience (UK DRI Annual Meeting)  
- **Public lecture:** Understanding neurodegeneration and brain donation research  
- **Guest seminar:** Ethics and ambition in experimental science
